cloy: verb. To fill up or choke up; to stop up.
col: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Columbia-Wenatchi.
colony: noun. A geographical area under the remote control of a country; especially to extract resources or exploit labor from that area.
coly: noun. Any bird of either of the genera Colius or Urocolius, endemic to sub-Saharan Africa.
common: adjective. Mutual; shared by more than one.
commonly: adverb. As a rule; frequently; usually.
con: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cofán.
cony: noun. A rabbit, especially the European rabbit, Oryctolagus cuniculus (formerly known as Lepus cuniculus).
coo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Comox.
cool: adjective. Of a mildly low temperature.
coy: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Coyaima.
loco: adverb. A direction in written or printed music to be returning to the proper pitch after having played an octave higher or lower.
loo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lombo.
loom: noun. A utensil; tool; a weapon; an article in general.
loon: noun. A crazy or deranged person; a lunatic.
moc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mocoví.
moly: noun. A magic herb or plant used by Odysseus to overcome Circe.
mom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Monimbo.
mon: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mongolian.
mono: noun. A bicycle or motorcycle trick where the front wheel is lifted off the ground while riding.
moo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Monom.
mool: noun. Alternative form of mold (“soil, earth”).
moon: proper noun. Alternative letter-case form of Moon (“the Earth 's only permanent natural satellite”).
nolo: adjective. Having no or low alcohol content; being low-alcohol or alcohol-free
nom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nocamán.
yom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yombe.
yon: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yongkom.
cly: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eastern Highland Chatino.
loc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Inonhan.
loy: noun. A type of one-eared spade used in Ireland.
momo: noun. A type of dumpling from Nepal, Ladakh or Tibet made with a simple flour and water dough and filled with meat, vegetables or cheese.
mooly: adjective. Alternative form of muley (“without horns”).
myc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mayeka.
mym: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Me'en.
nco: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sibe.
nol: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nomlaki.
noy: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Noy.
olm: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Oloma.
oom: noun. An older man, especially an uncle. (Frequently as a respectful form of address.)
oon: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Önge.
